summary.p: #109. a) Tiruvālantuṟai Mahādeva temple; b) on the round part of the base (kumuda) of the northern façade of the ardha-maṇḍapa and the sanctuary; c) personally located and read in situ; d) ARE 1926, no. 227; e) lost regnal year of Kōppara{{kesarivarman}}; f) Rājendra I; g) inscription read with G. Vijayavenugopal; h) lines 1–2 contain the meykkīrtti of Rājendra I.

revisionDesc.change: Further conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)
Conversion to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p: Fortune! Prosperity! {meykkīrtti} Having given (kuṭuttu) a land (nilam)It is difficult to make sense of the wordcoṟṟi / cōṟṟi, which is after nilam. I thus did not attempt to translate it. [to] the Lord (āḻvār) of Tiruvālantuṟai of Ciṟupaḻuvūr, a brahmadeya of Kuṉṟakkūṟṟam alias Uttuṅka{{tuṅkavaḷa}}nāṭu; from the kācus (kācil) which stay (irinta > irunta) in the accountant office (pāṇṭāram), we the Śrīkkōyiluṭaiyars of this temple (ikkōyil) took (koṇṭa) 15 kācus; having taken (koṇṭu) these fiteen (patiṉ aiñtum > aiñcum) kācus, to graciously make (ceytaruḷa) three handfuls (mūppiṭi) of ghee food offerings (neyyamutu) everyday (nicatam) as [i.e. at the rate of] (āka) one handful (oru piṭi) for one time (pōtu) of ghee food offerings (neyyamutu).I cannot make sense ofcōmittāl or cōḻittāl. May it be a kind of measure for the ghee? This is under the protection of the Panmāheśvaras, as long as the sun and the moon endure.
Edited and translated in , based on autopsy and photographs (by Valérie Gillet, 2016-2018).
This digital edition by Valérie Gillet and Emmanuel Francis.
